mappings.rationaleAI: The Antikythera Mechanism's intricate bronze gear system for calculating celestial positions exemplifies ancient machine mechanisms (B.2) and precision gearing (B.3.a), demonstrating sophisticated mechanical engineering capability over two thousand years ago.

Automata from ancient Greek designs through Enlightenment clockwork embody the development of machine mechanisms (B.2) employing simple machines (B.1), gears, cams, and linkages (B.3) to produce lifelike mechanical motion, tracing the evolution of precision machine components across cultures.

Centuries of perpetual motion machine attempts, from Leonardo da Vinci's spinning wheels to Boyle's fluid systems, directly concern machine mechanisms (B.2), simple machines such as the wheel and lever (B.1), and the fundamental role of friction in limiting machine operation (B.4).

The Stephensons' locomotive engineering, including the Rocket, involved designing complex machine mechanisms (B.2) integrating components such as linkages, bearings, shafts, couplings, and brakes (B.3) that established foundational principles for mechanical engineering in powered machinery.

Pitt-Rivers organized thousands of tools and artifacts typologically to demonstrate incremental technological evolution from primitive to complex forms, directly documenting the early history and developmental sequence of hand tools (A.1).
